title: Slipping or Just Choosing Something Else
excerpt: I missed the final assignment for Art of Why. It's 20% of the grade. I'll probably barely pass now.
content:
What happened

I missed the final assignment for Art of Why. It's 20% of the grade. I'll probably barely pass now.

This is a class I actually liked. I participated a lot. I care about the material.

I just didn't realize the assignment was due.

---

What I'm telling people

I'm overloaded. Recruiting is intense. I've been focused on getting a job.

That's what I told the professor too.

---

What's probably actually true

I've made a quiet decision that recruiting matters more than school.

Not explicitly. But in how I spend time, what I check, what I ignore.

I say I was recruiting all weekend. That's partially true. But I also spent time with friends and went out.

So it's not just pressure. It's allocation.

And I'm not being fully honest about that.

---

What I'm feeling but not saying

This shouldn't bother me as much as it does.

But it does because it feels familiar.

In college I was always a little off. Late. Missing things. One step behind. Someone said that about me once and it stuck.

I thought I'd fixed that.

So now when something slips, it doesn't feel like a one-off. It feels like I'm reverting.

And I hate that.

There's also something uncomfortable about how I explained it. I framed it in a way that sounds responsible, but I know I edited the truth.

That bothers me more than the missed assignment.

---

Open questions

Am I actually overwhelmed, or am I just choosing what matters and avoiding owning that tradeoff?

Why do small mistakes hit so hard when they connect to old versions of myself?

If I'm this focused on recruiting, what else am I okay letting slip?

Am I in control right now, or just reacting to pressure?
